patterns.name: Two Pointers — Opposite Ends
patterns.shortDescription: Two pointers starting at both ends, converging toward the middle
patterns.whenToUse: Sorted array, palindrome check, reverse in place, pair sum on sorted input

slots.label: compare or process arr[left] and arr[right]
move left++ or right-- based on logic

patterns.name: Two Pointers — Same Direction (Slow/Fast)
patterns.shortDescription: Slow read pointer + fast write pointer (or different speeds), both moving forward
patterns.whenToUse: In-place array filtering/dedup, reading at one rate while writing at another, cycle detection

slots.label: decide whether to keep current fast value
if keep, write to slow position; advance slow
return slow (often new length or transformed value)

patterns.name: Two Pointers — Merge
patterns.shortDescription: Two pointers across two sorted inputs, advancing whichever is smaller
patterns.whenToUse: Merging sorted arrays/lists, intersection/union of sorted sequences

slots.label: i, j for each input; result array

patterns.name: Sliding Window — Variable Size
patterns.shortDescription: Window expands by moving right, shrinks by moving left when invariant violated
patterns.whenToUse: Longest/shortest substring with constraint, max sum subarray with constraint

slots.label: while invariant violated, remove arr[left] and left++
update best result with current window

patterns.name: Sliding Window — Fixed Size k
patterns.shortDescription: Window of fixed size k slides across the array; maintain rolling stat
patterns.whenToUse: Max/min/sum of every contiguous subarray of size k

slots.label: build first window of size k; capture initial stat
update best with new window's stat

patterns.name: Hash Map — Frequency Counter
patterns.shortDescription: Build a Map of element → count; query the map
patterns.whenToUse: Anagram check, character frequency, finding most/least common

patterns.name: Hash Set — Membership Check
patterns.shortDescription: Build a Set of seen items; check membership in O(1)
patterns.whenToUse: Detect duplicates, set intersection/union, check if element exists

patterns.name: Hash Map — Complement Lookup
patterns.shortDescription: For each item, look up its complement (target - item) in the map
patterns.whenToUse: Two Sum and variants — pair-finding where you know what to look for

slots.label: if not found, store current value

patterns.name: Stack — Matching/Validation
patterns.shortDescription: Push openers; pop and check match on closers
patterns.whenToUse: Brackets/parentheses validation, undo operations

slots.label: empty stack; map of pairs if needed
if opener push; if closer pop and verify

patterns.name: Stack — Monotonic
patterns.shortDescription: Maintain stack in monotonic order; pop while invariant violated
patterns.whenToUse: Next greater/smaller element, daily temperatures, largest rectangle, span problems

slots.label: while stack top violates monotonic order, pop

patterns.name: Linked List — Dummy + Predecessor
patterns.shortDescription: Use dummy head + curr-as-predecessor pattern; check curr.next, mutate or advance
patterns.whenToUse: Delete nodes, insert nodes, any operation that needs the node BEFORE the target

slots.label: dummy = { val: 0, next: head }; curr = dummy

patterns.name: Linked List — Two Pointers (Slow/Fast)
patterns.shortDescription: Slow advances 1 step, fast advances 2 — Floyd's cycle detection style
patterns.whenToUse: Cycle detection, find middle, find nth from end

slots.label: check meeting condition or pointer state

patterns.name: Linked List — Reverse In-Place
patterns.shortDescription: Iterate with prev/curr/next; flip pointer each step
patterns.whenToUse: Reverse a list, reverse sub-list, reorder list

slots.label: curr.next = prev (or swap prev/next for doubly-linked)

patterns.name: Binary Search — Standard
patterns.shortDescription: Find an exact value (or report not found) in a sorted array
patterns.whenToUse: Sorted array; need to find a specific value or position

slots.label: if equal return; if less lo = mid+1; else hi = mid-1

patterns.name: Binary Search — Boundary (First True)
patterns.shortDescription: Find the first index where a predicate becomes true (or last where it's false)
patterns.whenToUse: First/last occurrence, leftmost/rightmost insertion point, transition point

slots.label: if predicate(mid) hi = mid; else lo = mid + 1

patterns.name: Binary Search — On Answer Space
patterns.shortDescription: Binary search over the answer values themselves; check feasibility at mid
patterns.whenToUse: Capacity-style problems: find min/max value such that feasibility check passes

slots.label: lo, hi = bounds of possible answers
if feasible(mid) hi = mid; else lo = mid + 1

patterns.name: Tree — DFS (Recursive)
patterns.shortDescription: Recursive function processes a node, then recurses on left and right
patterns.whenToUse: Max depth, validate BST, path sum, any tree property derivable from subtrees

slots.label: combine node's value with left/right results
return combined value to parent call

patterns.name: Tree — BFS (Level Order)
patterns.shortDescription: Process tree level by level using a queue
patterns.whenToUse: Level traversal, right side view, level-aware computations

slots.label: iterate size nodes; collect/process per level
push node.left and node.right if present

patterns.name: Graph — Grid DFS
patterns.shortDescription: DFS on a 2D grid, marking visited cells in place or with set
patterns.whenToUse: Number of islands, flood fill, connected components on grid

slots.label: out of bounds OR cell !== target → return
mark visited (overwrite cell or add to set)
for each cell, if matches: dfs and increment counter

patterns.name: Graph — Grid BFS (Shortest Path)
patterns.shortDescription: BFS on a 2D grid using a queue, tracking distance
patterns.whenToUse: Shortest path on grid, level-by-level grid expansion (rotting oranges, walls and gates)

slots.label: queue with start + distance; visited set/grid
if passable and unvisited: mark and enqueue with dist+1

patterns.name: Topological Sort (Kahn's BFS)
patterns.shortDescription: Process nodes in dependency order using indegree + queue
patterns.whenToUse: Course schedule, build order, alien dictionary, dependency resolution

slots.label: build adjacency list and indegree map
decrement neighbors' indegree; enqueue if hits 0
result if length matches node count, else cycle exists

patterns.name: Union Find (DSU)
patterns.shortDescription: Disjoint set union with path compression and union by rank
patterns.whenToUse: Connected components, redundant connection, account merge, dynamic connectivity

slots.label: find compresses path; union links by rank
count distinct roots or query specific connectivity

patterns.name: DP — 1D
patterns.shortDescription: dp[i] depends on a small fixed number of previous values
patterns.whenToUse: Climbing stairs, fibonacci, house robber, decode ways

slots.label: set dp[0], dp[1] (or whichever indices are base)
for i from base+1 to n

patterns.name: DP — 2D Grid
patterns.shortDescription: dp[i][j] depends on dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1] (or similar neighbors)
patterns.whenToUse: Unique paths, min path sum, maximal square, cherry pickup

slots.label: initialize first row and first column

patterns.name: DP — Sequence (LCS-style)
patterns.shortDescription: dp[i][j] over two strings/arrays; compares chars at i, j
patterns.whenToUse: Longest common subsequence, edit distance, longest palindromic subsequence

slots.label: if s1[i-1] === s2[j-1] dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1; else max/min

patterns.name: DP — 0/1 Knapsack
patterns.shortDescription: Choose subset of items to maximize value within capacity
patterns.whenToUse: Partition equal subset sum, coin change variants, target sum

slots.label: outer: for each item; inner: for c from capacity down to item.weight

patterns.name: DP — Interval
patterns.shortDescription: dp[i][j] over interval (i,j); inner loop over split point k
patterns.whenToUse: Burst balloons, matrix chain multiplication, palindrome partitioning

slots.label: dp = n × n table; pad input with sentinels if helpful
outer: for length 1..n; inner: for i (j = i + length - 1); innermost: for k = i..j
dp[i][j] = max/min over splits at k

patterns.name: DP — State Machine (Decision)
patterns.shortDescription: Track multiple dp states (e.g., holding/not-holding stock); transition between states each step
patterns.whenToUse: Stock buy/sell variants with cooldown, transaction limits, paint house

slots.label: one variable per state (e.g., hold, cash) or dp[i][state]
init each state's value at step 0 (e.g., hold = -prices[0], cash = 0)
compute next state values from current; update

patterns.name: Backtracking — Generate All
patterns.shortDescription: Recursively build candidates; choose, recurse, undo (push/pop)
patterns.whenToUse: Permutations, combinations, subsets, generate parentheses

slots.label: if path satisfies completion criteria, push to result and return
for each choice in remaining options

patterns.name: Heap — Top K
patterns.shortDescription: Maintain a heap of size k while scanning items
patterns.whenToUse: K largest, K closest, top K frequent

slots.label: if heap size > k, pop smallest

patterns.name: Intervals — Sort + Merge
patterns.shortDescription: Sort intervals by start; iterate and merge overlapping
patterns.whenToUse: Merge intervals, insert interval, meeting rooms

slots.label: sort intervals by start time; result = [intervals[0]]
if overlaps with last in result, extend end; else push as new

patterns.name: Greedy — Sort + Linear Scan
patterns.shortDescription: Sort first; iterate making the locally optimal choice each step
patterns.whenToUse: Interval scheduling, merge cost minimization, partition labels

slots.label: sort by relevant criterion; init accumulator
for each item in sorted order

patterns.name: Greedy — Running Max/Min Tracking
patterns.shortDescription: Single pass; maintain running min/max and update answer greedily
patterns.whenToUse: Best time to buy stock, jump game, gas station

patterns.name: Greedy + Heap
patterns.shortDescription: Use heap to always pick the optimal element greedily
patterns.whenToUse: Task scheduler, reorganize string, connect ropes, minimum cost merge

slots.label: insert all elements into heap (max or min)
push back any modified element if applicable

patterns.name: Sort — Implementations (Pedagogical)
patterns.shortDescription: Implement a sorting algorithm from scratch (bubble, insertion, selection, merge, quick)
patterns.whenToUse: Drilling the mechanics of sorting algorithms; understanding time/space tradeoffs

slots.label: outer pass over array (n-1 passes typical)
inner loop to compare/swap or select
return sorted array (in place or new)

patterns.name: Sort — Custom Comparator
patterns.shortDescription: Use built-in sort with a custom comparison function
patterns.whenToUse: Sort by frequency, multi-key sort, lexicographic with twist (largest number)

slots.label: optional: build frequency map / derive sort key per element
optional: derive final result from sorted order

patterns.name: Sort + Linear Scan
patterns.shortDescription: Sort first, then iterate to find pairs/sequences/answer
patterns.whenToUse: Problems easier after sorting: arithmetic progression, min difference pairs, max product of K

slots.label: sort the input; init accumulator/best result

patterns.name: Graph — BFS/DFS on Adjacency List
patterns.shortDescription: BFS or DFS over a graph represented as an adjacency list (or implicit state graph)
patterns.whenToUse: Generic graph traversal where neighbors come from a Map/array (not a grid)

slots.label: build adjacency list; visited set; queue/stack with start
if unvisited, mark and enqueue with new state

patterns.name: Dijkstra — Weighted Shortest Path
patterns.shortDescription: Min-heap based shortest path on weighted graphs (non-negative weights)
patterns.whenToUse: Shortest path with edge weights; cheapest flights with K stops; network delay time

slots.label: pop smallest [d, node]; skip if d > dist[node]
for each neighbor: if d + weight < dist[next], update and push

patterns.name: Tarjan — Bridges / Articulation Points
patterns.shortDescription: DFS with discovery/low-link tracking to find critical edges/vertices
patterns.whenToUse: Critical connections, articulation points, bridges in a graph

slots.label: if disc[v] !== -1 (already visited), update low and skip
for unvisited neighbor v: dfs(v, u); set disc, low
if low[v] > disc[u], record edge (u,v) as bridge / mark articulation

patterns.name: DP — Bitmask (Subset State)
patterns.shortDescription: DP where state is a bitmask representing which items have been used/visited
patterns.whenToUse: Traveling salesman variants, assignment problems with small N (≤20), subset enumeration with optimization

slots.label: for mask from 0 to (1 << n) - 1; for each unset bit i
dp[mask | (1 << i)] = min(dp[next], dp[mask] + cost(i, mask))

patterns.name: Trie — Prefix Tree Search
patterns.shortDescription: Build a trie of words; query with prefix or backtracking traversal
patterns.whenToUse: Word search II, autocomplete, longest common prefix, dictionary lookup

slots.label: insert all words char-by-char into nested children map
if cell out of bounds OR no child for ch, return
if trie node marks end-of-word, record match (and clear to dedupe)
DFS in 4 directions (or descend the trie further)
if trie subtree empty, can drop; backtrack mark on cell

patterns.name: Sweep Line
patterns.shortDescription: Sort events by position; sweep across maintaining an active set
patterns.whenToUse: Skyline problem, meeting rooms II, calendar booking, interval coverage

slots.label: convert intervals into start/end events; sort events
update active set / running count based on event type
return result (peak count, transition list, etc.)

patterns.name: Divide and Conquer
patterns.shortDescription: Split into smaller subproblems, solve recursively, combine results
patterns.whenToUse: Merge sort, quick sort, find majority element, max subarray (D&C version)

slots.label: single element / trivial input → return directly

patterns.name: Linear Scan
patterns.shortDescription: Single pass through input making a per-element decision; no auxiliary data structure
patterns.whenToUse: Find max/min, count satisfying condition, in-place transform, simple aggregations that don't need a Map/Set/Stack

slots.label: for each element (or index) in input

patterns.name: Data Structure Design
patterns.shortDescription: Build a class supporting a defined set of operations efficiently
patterns.whenToUse: LRU cache, median finder, trie, segment tree, design hit counter, twitter — any class-style problem with operation sequence

patterns.name: Event Simulation
patterns.shortDescription: Step through events/time/state transitions in order, updating world state per step
patterns.whenToUse: Game-of-life-style ticks, robot movement on a grid, parsing scripted commands, processing timeline of timestamped events

slots.label: apply event's effect to world state
return final state or aggregated result
